pathway-builder.title: Pathway Builder
pathway-builder.body: A CEA Pathway defines how a district evolves over time, which buildings are constructed, demolished, or retrofitted at each year. Create multiple Pathways to compare different development scenarios and the impact of different decarbonisation strategies.

pathway-viewer.title: Pathway Viewer
pathway-viewer.body: Only fully baked Pathways are shown here. Use Pathway Builder to create Pathways.

intervention-templates.title: Intervention Templates
intervention-templates.body: Reusable modification recipes (e.g. wall insulation upgrade, HVAC retrofit) that change building properties for selected Archetypes. Define once, apply to any year across any Pathway.

simulate-pathway.title: Simulate Pathway
simulate-pathway.body:
To enable, ensure:
1. Only one Pathway is checked
2. All states are baked (blue nodes)

This button facilitates batch processing of all States at one go. Consider manual simulation for each State when having complex input settings or simulation error persists.

add-building-event-or-intervention.title: Add a Building Event or Apply an Intervention
add-building-event-or-intervention.body: Enter a year, then choose an action. Select an intervention template to apply it directly. Clear the selection to add a building event instead.

bake-states.title: Bake States
bake-states.body: Bake materialises state folders from the pathway log. Run after creating building events or applying interventions. Required before simulation.

state-types.title: State Types
state-types.body:
Auto-Stock: Buildings with a construction year matching this state year.
Construct-Event: Buildings explicitly added in this year.
Demolish-Event: Buildings explicitly removed in this year.
Intervention: Building property modifications applied via intervention templates.

construction-card.title: Construction
construction-card.body: Buildings constructed in this state year. A building with (N) indicates it has been rebuilt N times after demolition.

demolition-card.title: Demolition
demolition-card.body: Buildings demolished in this state year.

intervention-card.title: Intervention
intervention-card.body: Building property modifications applied via intervention templates for the listed archetypes.

building-lifecycle.title: Building Event Timeline
building-lifecycle.body: Timeline of this building's construction and demolition events across checked pathways. Solid nodes indicate construction (Rebuilt(N) for rebuilds after demolition). Hollow nodes indicate demolition. Solid lines show active periods, dashed lines show gaps or pre-existence. Click a building pill in the inspector or a building on the map to view.

construction-archetypes-info.body: Construction Archetypes define the building envelope and systems standards. Assign in Input Editor > zone (const_type). Edit or add in Database Editor > CONSTRUCTION_ARCHETYPES.

main-use-type-info.body: Main use type is the use type with the highest occupancy ratio. Assign in Input Editor > zone (use_type1, use_type2, use_type3 and their ratios). Edit or add in Database Editor > USE_TYPES.

map-layer-range-mode.title: Total Range vs. Period Range
map-layer-range-mode.body:
Total Range - bar heights are normalised to each entity's full-year sum. Absolute scale; narrow periods look short.
Period Range - bar heights are normalised to the current period's max. Rescales with the slider for maximum contrast within the period.

canvas-sync-maps.title: Sync Maps
canvas-sync-maps.body:
On - every map card mirrors the overview map.
Off - each map card holds its own view and overlays.

canvas-fix-layout.title: Fix Layout
canvas-fix-layout.body:
On - card positions and sizes are locked; editing controls stay available.
Off - cards can be dragged and resized as usual.

canvas-enable-edit.title: Enable Edit
canvas-enable-edit.body:
On - editing controls are visible; the canvas is interactive.
Off - editing controls are hidden for a clean snapshot.

canvas-compare.title: Add Scenario to compare
canvas-compare.body:
Side-by-side comparison across scenarios.

The leftmost column is your "origin" — the only column where you can edit cards. Every other column mirrors the origin's structure but renders against its own scenario's data. Add or remove a card on the origin and every column reflects the change.

Up to 4 columns total (origin + 3 picks).

Click the `+` next to the scenario title to open the picker. Pre-fills with your last picks if you've compared before. Remove a column with the `×` in its title; removing the last comparison column folds back to the single-column view.

canvas-share.title: Share canvas
canvas-share.body:
Bundles the canvas folder into a .zip you can hand off.

The recipient gets the structure (layout, card picks, plot scripts) and - when they import it - your numbers re-render against their scenario. Two modes:
- Same scenario / what-if names in their project → full round-trip.
- Different names → template share. They keep the structure; columns referencing scenarios they don't have won't resolve.

canvas-import.title: Import canvas
canvas-import.body:
Unpacks a canvas .zip into the current scenario.

The canvas keeps the .zip's config (layout, card picks, plot scripts) but renders against the current scenario's data - donor's structure, your numbers.

If the .zip's column references match a scenario / what-if name in your project they resolve normally; otherwise those columns stay as a template placeholder.
